_description: Math UEPG concepts at grade 5: fraction operations, decimal operations, intro to ratios, basic statistics, coordinate plane

uepgs.title: Smart Decimal Arithmetic
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: calculation strategies for decimal expressions — common base factoring, complement to round numbers, two-level regrouping, ratio recognition between unusual decimals, and algebraic substitution
uepgs.skills_summary: common base factor (scaled decimals), complement to round number / missing piece, two-level regrouping, ratio/proportion recognition between unusual decimals, algebraic substitution for repeated groups, strategy selection

uepgs.title: System of Two Linear Equations
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: solving a system of two linear equations in two unknowns (x, y) — substitution, direct elimination, scale-then-eliminate, word problem setup, and solution verification
uepgs.skills_summary: system concepts and solution checking, substitution (coefficient-1 form, both x and y), direct elimination (equal coefficients), scale-then-eliminate (one or both equations), word problems (6 contexts: prices, tickets, two-number sum/diff, ages, fruit, speeds), strategy selection and explicit verification

uepgs.title: Mean, Median & Mode
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: three measures of centre — mode (most frequent), median (sorted middle), mean vs median comparison, effect of outliers, work-backwards to achieve a target mean or median, and choosing the right measure for a given context
uepgs.skills_summary: mode (single/bimodal/no-mode/trap), median (odd sort/even average), mean vs median comparison (skew), outlier effect on mean (New Mean = (A×N+outlier)÷(N+1)), work backwards for mean (x=target×N−knownSum) and median (x=2×target−neighbour), choose right measure (mean/median/mode)

uepgs.title: Combinatorial Counting
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: systematic counting strategies — multiplication principle for sequential independent choices, addition principle for mutually exclusive cases, pair/diagonal counting C(n,2), systematic enumeration of bills and digit restrictions, partitions with dividers (each≥1 and each≥2), and maximum regions by straight cuts
uepgs.skills_summary: multiplication principle (2-step, 3-step, repeated choices), addition principle (simple OR, OR-of-products single relay, OR-of-products dual relay), pair counting C(n,2)=n(n-1)/2 and diagonals n(n-3)/2, systematic enumeration (bills: (a+1)(b+1)-1; digit restrictions: constrained position first), partitions with dividers (each≥1: (n-1)(n-2)/2; each≥2: (n-4)(n-5)/2), max regions by cuts: n(n+1)/2+1

uepgs.title: Greatest Common Divisor (GCD)
uepgs.subject: number_theory
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: finding the greatest common divisor using three methods — listing all factors, prime factorization (minimum exponents of common primes), and the Euclidean algorithm (repeated division with remainder) — plus GCD word problems and coprime properties
uepgs.skills_summary: factors and divisibility (factor pairs, factor count, largest proper factor), GCD by listing (list factors, find common, pick greatest), GCD by prime factorization (factor tree, take minimum exponent of each common prime), GCD by Euclidean algorithm (a=q×b+r, repeat until r=0), GCD word problems (ribbon, tile, distribution, plank contexts), coprime numbers and GCD properties (GCD=1, factor-out property, special values)

uepgs.title: Least Common Multiple (LCM)
uepgs.subject: number_theory
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: finding the least common multiple using three methods — listing multiples, prime factorization (maximum exponents of all primes), and the GCD shortcut (LCM = a×b÷GCD) — plus the LCM×GCD identity, periodic word problems, and divisibility properties
uepgs.skills_summary: multiples and common multiples (kth multiple, NOT a multiple, first common multiple, count in range), LCM by listing (list multiples, find first common), LCM by prime factorization (factor tree, take maximum exponent of each prime), LCM via GCD shortcut (LCM = a×b÷GCD), LCM word problems (bus timing, lights, work cycles, recurring events), LCM properties (a|b→LCM=b, LCM×GCD=a×b identity, special value rules)

uepgs.title: LCM & GCD Combined
uepgs.subject: number_theory
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: competition-level integration of LCM and GCD — the identity LCM×GCD=a×b, reconstructing pairs from given GCD and LCM, extending to three numbers, word problems requiring both operations, ratio-based relationships, and ratio+GCD/LCM competition problems
uepgs.skills_summary: LCM×GCD=a×b identity (find LCM, GCD, product, or missing number), pair reconstruction (write a=gm b=gn with gcd(m,n)=1, count coprime factorizations of L/g), three-number GCD and LCM (apply two at a time), word problems (GCD for sharing, LCM for synchronization), LCM/GCD ratio and sum relations (LCM=k×GCD, LCM+GCD=S, square-root trick), ratio+GCD/LCM competition (LCM=m×n×g, GCD=LCM÷(mn), g=sqrt(P/mn))

uepgs.title: River Boat Problems
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: solving river boat (moving water) problems using the upstream/downstream speed model — setting up and solving one- and two-equation systems, comparing times, applying the water-frame trick, and using ratio relationships between speeds
uepgs.skills_summary: upstream/downstream model (v_down=v+u, v_up=v−u; recover v=(v_d+v_u)/2, u=(v_d−v_u)/2), one-direction equations (find v or u from distance and time), two-equation system (set up and solve for both v and u), time comparison (faster downstream, round-trip total), applied word problems (plane/wind context, two boats meeting at 2v closing speed, downstream-then-return time), competition tricks (water-frame drift u=d/(2T), upstream-then-return, same-time ratio v:u=(x+y):(x−y))

uepgs.title: Sub-Rectangle Counting
uepgs.subject: combinatorics
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: counting axis-aligned sub-rectangles in an n×m grid that contain one or more marked squares, using the boundary-choice formula and inclusion-exclusion for two- and three-point constraints
uepgs.skills_summary: single-point containment (r×(n-r+1)×c×(m-c+1)), both points via bounding box, A-not-B via subtraction, neither via inclusion-exclusion (Total-cA-cB+cAB), three-point A∪B-not-C via two-stage inclusion-exclusion, competition: exactly-one (cA+cB-2cAB), same-row factoring, three-point exactly-one formula
